description: A clipboard history manager CLI written in Go. Single binary, daemon + CLI commands, local storage under ~/.snip/. Production-quality: maintainable, tested, no shortcuts.
scope: Implement the tasks below. Each run: choose one undone task by priority (architectural and integration first, polish last); respect dependencies. Each task is done only when all acceptance criteria are satisfied. Do not skip or reinterpret criteria. Complete = all tasks have "done": true.

tasks.title: Project scaffolding
tasks.description: Initialize Go module, install dependencies (cobra, bbolt, clipboard, fuzzy, viper). Create main.go with cobra root command. Create cmd/ and internal/ directories. Verify `go build` works and `./snip --help` prints usage.
acceptance: go.mod exists with all dependencies
go build produces a snip binary
./snip --help prints usage info

tasks.title: Clipboard watcher daemon
tasks.description: Implement `internal/clipboard/watcher.go` — a loop that polls the system clipboard every 500ms, detects new content, and emits it via a channel. Implement `snip daemon` command that starts the watcher and prints each new clip to stdout (temporary, for testing). Handle graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
acceptance: snip daemon starts and watches clipboard
New copies are detected and printed to stdout
Ctrl+C shuts down cleanly
Unit tests for watcher logic exist and pass

tasks.title: BoltDB storage layer
tasks.description: Implement `internal/store/store.go` — functions to save a clip (with timestamp, content hash to deduplicate), list clips (newest first), get a clip by ID, delete a clip, and prune clips beyond a max count. Store location: ~/.snip/history.db
acceptance: Store creates ~/.snip/ directory if missing
Save, List, Get, Delete, Prune functions work
Duplicate content is not stored twice in a row
Unit tests for all store functions pass

tasks.title: Wire daemon to storage
tasks.description: Connect the clipboard watcher to the BoltDB store. When the daemon detects a new clip, save it to the store. The daemon should now run silently in the background (remove stdout printing). Add a --max-history flag (default 500) to control how many clips are kept.
acceptance: Daemon saves clips to BoltDB as they are copied
Old clips are pruned when max-history is exceeded
--max-history flag works
No stdout noise during normal operation

tasks.title: snip list command
tasks.description: Implement `snip list` — shows the N most recent clips in a formatted table: index, timestamp (relative, like '2m ago'), and a truncated preview of the content (max 80 chars). Default N=20, configurable with --count flag.
acceptance: snip list shows recent clips with index, time, preview
Long content is truncated with ellipsis
--count flag limits output
Empty history shows a friendly message

tasks.title: snip copy command
tasks.description: Implement `snip copy <index>` — copies the clip at the given index back to the system clipboard. Also support `snip copy --last` to re-copy the most recent entry. Print confirmation to stderr.
acceptance: snip copy 3 puts clip #3 on the clipboard
snip copy --last copies the newest clip
Invalid index shows a clear error
Confirmation message printed to stderr

tasks.title: snip search with fuzzy matching
tasks.description: Implement `snip search <query>` — fuzzy-searches all stored clips and displays matching results ranked by relevance. Use github.com/sahilm/fuzzy. Highlight the matched portion in the output using ANSI colors. Add --limit flag (default 10).
acceptance: snip search 'query' returns fuzzy-matched clips
Results are ranked by match quality
Matched characters are highlighted in output
--limit flag works
No results shows a friendly message

tasks.title: snip clear and snip delete commands
tasks.description: Implement `snip clear` — deletes all history after a confirmation prompt (skip with --force). Implement `snip delete <index>` — removes a single clip by index.
acceptance: snip clear asks for confirmation before deleting
snip clear --force skips confirmation
snip delete <index> removes one clip
Invalid index shows a clear error

tasks.title: Configuration file support
tasks.description: Add config file support via viper. Location: ~/.snip/config.yaml. Supported settings: max_history (int), poll_interval_ms (int), storage_path (string). CLI flags override config file values. Implement `snip config` command that prints current config.
acceptance: Config file is loaded from ~/.snip/config.yaml if present
CLI flags override config values
snip config prints current effective config
Missing config file uses sensible defaults
Unit tests for config loading pass

tasks.title: Daemon PID file and status
tasks.description: Add PID file management to the daemon (~/.snip/daemon.pid). Prevent multiple daemon instances. Implement `snip status` — shows whether daemon is running, PID, uptime, and clip count. Implement `snip stop` — sends SIGTERM to the running daemon.
acceptance: Only one daemon instance can run at a time
snip status shows daemon info or 'not running'
snip stop kills the daemon cleanly
PID file is cleaned up on exit

tasks.title: Polished output and error handling
tasks.description: Add color output using github.com/fatih/color. Consistent error messages with exit codes. Add --no-color global flag. Add --json flag to list and search for machine-readable output. Ensure all commands fail gracefully when daemon is not running.
acceptance: All output uses consistent color scheme
--no-color disables colors
--json outputs valid JSON for list and search
Commands that need the store fail gracefully with helpful messages
go vet ./... passes with no warnings

tasks.title: README and final polish
tasks.description: Write README.md with: project description, installation instructions (go install), usage examples for every command, config file reference, and architecture overview. Add --version flag. Ensure go test coverage is above 60%.
acceptance: README.md exists with install, usage, and config docs
snip --version prints version string
go test -cover reports > 60% coverage
go build succeeds with no warnings
go vet passes clean
